body:
Indonesia posts 0.37% deflation in May, with weak demand and limited stimulus raising fears of deeper slowdown and rising layoffs.

body: Indonesia recorded a 0.37% deflation in May, driven by falling food prices, including garlic and chili, as imports decreased by over 14% year-over-year.

body: Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) wants its trade with ASEAN to reach $180 billion by 2032.

title: BPK Helps Indonesia Avoid Rp 43 Trillion in Losses

title: Indonesia to Launch $9 Wage Subsidy for Low-Income Workers in June

title: No Real Shift Yet: Pertamina Says Energy Transition Remains ‘Energy Addition’
body: Current energy policies have focused more on expanding renewable energy capacity rather than reducing fossil fuel consumption.
